GamesList:
Rails Evolution Project

Rails is a computer implementation of a number of board games.
These games all have a railroad theme. They are collectively known as "18xx"
games due to the naming scheme used by many games in the genre.

Contributors:
Stefan Bleeck, Erik Vos, Stefan Frey, Freek Dijkstra, Scott Peterson, Adam Badura,
Phil Davies, Bill Rosgen, Martin Brumm, Chris Shaffer, Brett Lentz,
Jason Klappste, Marc Arndt.

All rights reserved by the respective owners of the original games.
No challenge to the original author's or publisher's rights, licensing, or status is intended.

1835 (Germany)

Designer: Michael Meier-Bachl
Publisher: Hans im Glück Verlag (1990)

Three variants have been implemented: Standard, Clemens, and Snake.
No options have been implemented for the variants, but the game will automatically
adjust to the selected variant based on the game options chosen at game start.
This ensures the game can be played in a championship with no differences. Playable, needs full stress testing.

1817

Designers: Craig Bartell, Tim Thomashow
Publisher: Deep Thought Games (2010)

The ultimate stock market game. U="1817"> Playable, needs full stress testing.

1870 - Railroading across the Trans Mississippi

Designer: Bill Dixon
Publisher: Mayfair Games, Inc. (1992, 1995)

Limitations:
working on it! s

1830: The Game of Railroads and Robber Barons

Designer: Francis Tresham and Bruce Shelley
Publisher: The Avalon Hill Game Company (1986)
Includes Pere Marquette variant by Federico Vellani and Coalfields variant by Alan R. Moon.

1837 (Austria)

Designer: Leonhard Orgler (1994)

Variants:
  • v1 ("Basegame", as described by JD Galt and clarified by Steve Thomas)
  • Romoth (v1, with some different prices)
  • v2 (as described by Lonny Orgler, 2015)
Mid-game player worth is calculated as follows:
Major and National shares as usual. Coal and Minor shares as the price of the related
Major (if started) or National company. Privates (mountain railways) worth is zero.

18Chesapeake - North East USA

Designer: Scott Petersen (2019)

A streamlined 1830-derivative featuring an export mechanic to ensure brisk pacing. Playable, needs full stress testing.

1825 Unit 1

Designer: Francis Tresham
Publisher: Hartland Trefoil Ltd. (1994)

Known Limitations:
  • Trains do not have to own a train and directors cannot fund the purchase of one.
  • Tile lays that send track off the board are legal in 1825; currently Rails blocks some edge placements.
  • Upgrades to pre-printed tiles are lays, not promotions.
  • Two tiles placed on your turn not being adjacent is currently not enforced.
  • Receivership and selling privates back to the bank are not implemented.

1826 - Railroading in France and Belgium

Designer: David G.D. Hecht (1996, 2000)

Not yet fully developed or tested:
  • Share selling in emergency train buying.
  • Bankruptcy mechanics.
  • TGV running over track used by other trains.
Note: Players should check calculated H-train revenues and correct using the spinner if necessary.

1851 - The Railroads come to Kentucky & Tennessee

Designers: Mark Derrick and Chris Lawson

Limitation:
No random assignment of private companies (players should do this off-line and enter the result). Under construction.

1856 - Railroading in Upper Canada

Designer: Bill Dixon
Publisher: Mayfair Games, Inc. (1992, 1995)

Limitations:
  • The restriction that shares cannot be sold in the same round they are bought is not enforced.
  • The alternate destinations for VA and TGB are not shown correctly (only one hex).
Under construction.

1862

Prototype phase. Core mechanics pending implementation.

1880 - China

Designers: Leonhard Orgler and Helmut Ohley
Publisher: Double O Games / Mayfair Games, Inc. (2010)

1889 - History of Shikoku Railways

Designer: Yasutaka Ikeda (2003-2006)
Publisher: Wild Heaven Productions and Deep Thought Games

Limitation:
Privates cannot be traded between players.

18AL - The Railroads come to Alabama

Designer: Mark Derrick (2000, 2004)

Both the original and the final version (with/without delayed 4-train obsolescence) are implemented.

Limitation:
Privates cannot be traded between players.

18EU - A century of railroading in Europe

Designer: David G.D. Hecht (1997-2004)
Publisher: Deep Thought Games, LLC (2004)

Game variants:
  • With 4 players, add a 16th minor (Hamburg or Frankfurt).
  • Allow minors to upgrade home base to green (proposed by Bruce Lavoie).

18GA - The Railroads Come to Georgia

Designers: Mark Derrick and John David Galt (2000, 2007)

18Kaas (Netherlands)

Publisher: ERJO GAMES (1992)

Utilizes the same core restrictions as 1830. still to do: here is a new map and three new tiles: The green Bt tile 52 and the brown B+ tile 61 may b y placed o n G 9 only. The brown U (Utrecht)-tile 72 may b e placed o n T13 only. Tile 6 3 may not be placed on I13. Bremen/Hannover (C21 and G21), Engeland (J4 and L.2), and Ruhrgebied (J18 and K19) belong t o foreign countries. The Ruhrgebied (J18 and K19) has no value by itself, instead it doubles the value of all other towns on the route, and it counts as town. Alternate rule: Ruhrgebied (J18 and K19) has n o value by itself, instead it inercases the value of all other towns o n the route by 20/30, and it counts as town. You may not build on G13 (the hex cast of NY) until the green phase. Disregard the yellow in F14 (the yellow bordered hex with the small town i n it). The yellow preprinted NY-tile on Gll i s different from the NY-tile in 1830 and can be upgraded i n two different ways.

18Lummer - Lummerland

Designer: Kay-Viktor Stegemann

Limitations:
  • Frau Waas baking Gugelhupf not implemented.
  • Bidding for start position not implemented.

18NL - Niederland (Netherlands)

Designer: Helmut Ohley (1999)

Utilizes the same core restrictions as 1830.

18Scan - Railroading under the Northern Lights

Designer: David G.D. Hecht (2004-2005)
Publisher: Deep Thought Games, LLC (2005)

Limitations:
  • The bonus token owner is not asked for permission to buy a token; address off-game.
  • Similar to 1856, players connecting a Minor to its destination must manually report via the Special menu.

18TN - The Railroads Come to Tennessee

Designer: Mark Derrick (1996, 2006)
Publisher: Deep Thought Games, LLC

18VA

Prototype phase. Core mechanics pending implementation.

Steam over Holland

Designer: Bart van Dijk (2007)
Publisher: Vendetta Games

Initial Round Scenarios (Selectable Options):
  • Auction: Random privates drawn. 18EU-style auction follows (Default).
  • Deal: Random privates drawn and dealt, one to each player for base price.
  • Select: Each player chooses and buys any private for base price.
